mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at the effective lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and boosts present values of future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals a tight labor market and resilient consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in the majority of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment capture synchronous consumer demand and labor income strength; this combined impulse generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444926 and PAYEMS at 141679 as of Nov 2020
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns do not exceed the unconditional median return (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals manufacturing momentum that drives cyclical earnings revisions upward; this positive linkage generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in post-shock recovery regimes when output gaps are closing.
failure_condition: During periods classified as post-shock recovery, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.1 million units reflect durable goods demand and construction sector strength, which propagates into broad equity earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1100, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in the majority of triggered months over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE combined with month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth below 0.2 percent captures non-inflationary labor market tightening; this joint condition generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% and CPIAUCSL at 235.982 as of Nov 2020
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE declines and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth is below 0.2%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns do not outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation raises the probability of future monetary policy tightening even when the federal funds rate is currently low; this acceleration compresses forward earnings multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth captures the aggregate output impulse that drives top-line revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents; positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.
